body:
Fixes #104 ## Summary - Keep Google Docs soft line breaks outside inline bold/italic/strike/link markup in both JS and Rust HTML renderers, so marked multi-line runs serialize as per-line marked segments around `
`. - Keep Markdown markers balanced across embedded newlines in both renderers. - Align Rust browser-model parsing with JS by treating Google Docs soft breaks as unstyled separator nodes, preventing styled runs from spanning the break in the parsed block model. - Preserve Rust browser-model image `width` and `height` metadata and emit matching `` attributes in rendered HTML. - Add the issue 104 fixture, expected HTML/Markdown outputs, before/after logs, and case-study notes under `docs/case-studies/issue-104/`. ## Reproduction The shared fixture `docs/case-studies/issue-104/fixtures/multiline-marked-inline-image-model.json` reproduces the failing editor-model shape: a bold multi-line paragraph with soft breaks and an inline image between marked lines. Before the fix: - JS direct rendering emitted `Alpha
Beta` for a styled text node with an embedded newline. - Rust parsed the first two bold soft-broken lines into one styled text node and emitted `Line one
Line two`. - Rust omitted browser-model image dimensions from ``. After the fix, both CLIs match `multiline-marked-inline-image.expected.html` and keep markdown markers per line. ## Verification - `cd js && npm test -- --runTestsByPath tests/unit/gdocs.test.js --runInBand` - `cd js && npm run check` - `cd js && npm run format:check` - `cd js && npm test -- --runInBand --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/unit/browser.test.js --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/integration/browser-engines.test.js --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/integration/habr-article.test.js --testPathIgnorePatterns=tests/e2e/docker.test.js` - `node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs` - `cd rust && cargo test --test integration gdocs:: -- --nocapture` - `cd rust && cargo fmt --all -- --check` - `cd rust && cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` - `cd rust && cargo test` - `git diff --check` A broader JS Jest run excluding only Docker was also attempted and is preserved in `docs/case-studies/issue-104/js-test-non-docker.log`; it reached browser integration suites and failed because the local Playwright Chromium binary is not installed. The hermetic non-browser/non-Docker suite passed separately.
title: Fix Google Docs browser-model HTML parity
